title: Deployment
content:
### Building for Production

```bash
ng build --configuration production
# or
ng build --prod  # Deprecated in Angular 15+
```

### Build Output

The build output is placed in the `dist/` directory:

```
dist/my-app/
├── index.html
├── main.[hash].js         # Main application bundle
├── polyfills.[hash].js    # Polyfills bundle
├── styles.[hash].css      # Global styles
├── runtime.[hash].js      # Webpack runtime
├── vendor.[hash].js       # Vendor libraries (optional with Ivy)
└── assets/                # Static assets
```

Each lazy-loaded module produces a separate chunk file.

### Configuration Environments

Angular uses file replacement for environment-specific configuration:

```typescript
// src/environments/environment.ts (development defaults)
export const environment = {
  production: false,
  apiUrl: 'http://localhost:3000/api'
};

// src/environments/environment.prod.ts
export const environment = {
  production: true,
  apiUrl: 'https://api.example.com'
};
```

In `angular.json`:
```json
"configurations": {
  "production": {
    "fileReplacements": [
      {
        "replace": "src/environments/environment.ts",
        "with": "src/environments/environment.prod.ts"
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

### base href

Set the base URL for the application using `--base-href`:

```bash
ng build --base-href /my-app/
```

Or update in `index.html`:
```html
<base href="/my-app/">
```

### Deployment to Firebase

```bash
npm install -g firebase-tools
firebase init hosting
ng build --configuration production
firebase deploy --only hosting
```

Configure `firebase.json` for SPA fallback:
```json
{
  "hosting": {
    "public": "dist/my-app",
    "rewrites": [{
      "source": "**",
      "destination": "/index.html"
    }]
  }
}
```

### Deployment to Netlify

```bash
ng build --configuration production
# Drag dist/my-app to Netlify or use CLI:
netlify deploy --prod --dir dist/my-app
```

Create `netlify.toml` or `_redirects` for SPA fallback:
```
/*    /index.html   200
```

### Deployment to AWS S3

```bash
ng build --configuration production
aws s3 sync dist/my-app s3://my-bucket --delete
```

Configure S3 bucket for static hosting and add redirection rule:
```json
{
  "ErrorDocument": {
    "Key": "index.html"
  },
  "IndexDocument": {
    "Suffix": "index.html"
  },
  "RoutingRules": [{
    "Condition": {
      "HttpErrorCodeReturnedEquals": "404"
    },
    "Redirect": {
      "ReplaceKeyPrefixWith": "#!/"
    }
  }]
}
```

### SPA Fallback Configuration

For SPAs, all routes must serve `index.html` to allow Angular routing to work:

**Apache (.htaccess):**
```apache
RewriteEngine On
RewriteCond %{DOCUMENT_ROOT}%{REQUEST_URI} -f [OR]
RewriteCond %{DOCUMENT_ROOT}%{REQUEST_URI} -d
RewriteRule ^ - [L]
RewriteRule ^ /index.html
```

**Nginx:**
```nginx
location / {
  try_files $uri $uri/ /index.html;
}
```

**IIS (web.config):**
```xml
<system.webServer>
  <rewrite>
    <rules>
      <rule name="Angular Routes" stopProcessing="true">
        <match url=".*" />
        <conditions logicalGrouping="MatchAll">
          <add input="{REQUEST_FILENAME}" matchType="IsFile" negate="true" />
          <add input="{REQUEST_FILENAME}" matchType="IsDirectory" negate="true" />
        </conditions>
        <action type="Rewrite" url="/index.html" />
      </rule>
    </rules>
  </rewrite>
</system.webServer>
```
codeExample:
ng build --configuration production --base-href /app/
# Output: dist/app/
# Deploy dist/app contents to web server
# Configure server to return index.html for all routes (SPA fallback)

quiz.question: Which file in the build output should be served for all routes in an SPA?
How do you configure different API URLs for development and production?
options: Using environment.ts files with file replacement
Using environment variables in the browser
